title: Who Are You Online With AI?

modules.title: The Post Nobody Meant to Go Viral
modules.sub: Tracing how one post sparks an unexpected online chain reaction
modules.description: Learners step into a scenario where a casual social media post suddenly explodes in reach, and they must figure out why it spread so fast. By mapping the journey of the post, they discover how AI recommendation systems quietly decide what gets pushed to more eyes.

modules.title: When AI Amplifies What You Say
modules.sub: Seeing how algorithms turn small voices into loud signals
modules.description: Learners explore how AI systems score and boost content based on engagement, often regardless of intent or accuracy. They make decisions about what to post, share, or ignore, then watch how the algorithm responds and reflects those choices back at scale.

modules.title: Responsibility in a World of Algorithms
modules.sub: Deciding who is accountable when AI drives the harm
modules.description: Learners are presented with real-style scenarios where amplified content causes hurt, and they must assign responsibility across creators, platforms, and algorithms. This module pushes them to think critically about the ethics of building and using systems that automate reach.

modules.title: Communities, Bias, and Who Gets Heard
modules.sub: Uncovering whose voices AI systems amplify or silence
modules.description: Learners investigate how recommendation algorithms can reflect and reinforce existing biases, favouring some communities and marginalising others. They analyse example feeds and comment sections to identify patterns in who gets promoted and who gets buried.

modules.title: Standing Up When AI Gets It Wrong
modules.sub: Practising how to act when algorithms cause real harm
modules.description: Learners face scenarios where AI systems spread misinformation, target vulnerable users, or silence important voices, and they must decide how to respond. They explore tools for reporting, pushing back, and advocating for change both as individuals and as part of a community.

modules.title: Draft Your Digital Citizenship Pledge
modules.sub: Writing your personal code of conduct for the algorithmic age
modules.description: Learners synthesise everything from the course to write their own digital citizenship pledge that accounts for AI amplification and platform responsibility. They make deliberate choices about language and commitments, producing a personalised document that reflects their values in an algorithm-driven world.

synopsis: One post. One algorithm. One chain of consequences you didn't expect â this course drops you into the middle of a social media spiral powered by AI recommendation systems and asks you to make the calls that shape what happens next. You'll leave having written your own digital citizenship code for the AI age.
rationale: AI-driven platforms have fundamentally changed what it means to act responsibly online, yet most digital citizenship curricula predate the algorithmic amplification era and leave students without frameworks for navigating it.
learning_outcome: Students can explain how AI recommendation systems shape online behavior and community norms, and can articulate a personal code of conduct that accounts for algorithmic amplification and platform responsibility.
source_signals: Digital Citizenship: Teaching Teens to Navigate the online World Responsibly | BWWT Ep. 240
Tali Horowitz: The Key Issue with AI Chatbots and Teens
Tali Horowitz: How Teens Are Really Using AI
The Risks of AI in Schools Outweigh the Benefits, Report Says
